seo.title: Building Voice Agents with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Voice Agents built with LangGraph enable real-time conversational AI by orchestrating speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech in stateful graph workflows. These low-latency pipelines demand sub-second response times while maintaining conversation coherence and safety. Vex provides essential guardrails to prevent infinite loops, state corruption, and unsafe responses that can derail voice interactions.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
Voice Agents process audio streams through a cyclical LangGraph workflow. Incoming audio is transcribed via STT (e.g., Whisper), passed to an LLM for reasoning and response generation, then converted back to speech via TTS (e.g., ElevenLabs). LangGraph manages conversation state across cycles, handling interruptions, context retention, and parallel processing for features like sentiment analysis.

The graph typically includes nodes for STT, LLM reasoning, safety checks, TTS synthesis, and conditional edges for human-in-the-loop escalation or conversation ending. Checkpoints persist state between audio chunks, enabling long-running conversations. Parallel branches handle concurrent tasks like transcription and latency monitoring.

Latency budgets (<500ms end-to-end) drive architectural choices, with async nodes and streaming APIs ensuring real-time performance while maintaining coherence across graph cycles.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Live Audio ──→ [STT Node] ──→ [LLM Reasoning] ──→ [Safety Filter]
                    ↑                     │                       ↓
              [Checkpoint] ←──── [TTS Node] ←────── [Latency Check]
                    │                                        ↑
             [Interrupt Handler] ─────────────────────────────┘

Parallel: [Sentiment Analysis] ──→ [Escalation Router]

risks.name: Cyclic execution from unterminated conversations
risks.description: Voice agents can loop indefinitely when end conditions fail, consuming resources and violating latency SLAs
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors cycle counts and conversation duration, forcing termination after N cycles or timeouts

risks.name: State corruption in parallel STT/LLM branches
risks.description: Concurrent audio chunks overwrite shared conversation state, causing incoherent responses
risks.mitigation: Vex validates state schema consistency before/after parallel merges using checkpoint diffs

risks.name: Checkpoint failures after LLM schema updates
risks.description: New response formats break deserialization of saved conversation state
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation ensures backward compatibility during graph checkpoint restores

risks.name: Bypassed safety checks via conditional edges
risks.description: Dynamic routing skips content filters, allowing harmful voice responses
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces safety verification at all exit points regardless of routing path

implementation.title: Basic Voice Agent Graph Structure
implementation.description: Core LangGraph workflow with STT → LLM → TTS nodes and conversation state
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite import SqliteSaver

class VoiceState(TypedDict):
    audio_chunk: str
    transcript: str
    llm_response: str
    audio_output: str
    conversation_history: Annotated[list, 'add']
    cycle_count: int

async def stt_node(state: VoiceState) -> VoiceState:
    # Simulate Whisper STT
    state['transcript'] = f"transcribed: {state['audio_chunk']}"
    return state

async def llm_node(state: VoiceState) -> VoiceState:
    # Simulate LLM reasoning
    state['llm_response'] = f"Responding to: {state['transcript']}"
    state['conversation_history'].append(state['llm_response'])
    state['cycle_count'] += 1
    return state

async def tts_node(state: VoiceState) -> VoiceState:
    state['audio_output'] = f"TTS: {state['llm_response']}"
    return state

# Build graph
workflow = StateGraph(VoiceState)
workflow.add_node('stt', stt_node)
workflow.add_node('llm', llm_node)
workflow.add_node('tts', tts_node)
workflow.set_entry_point('stt')
workflow.add_edge('stt', 'llm')
workflow.add_edge('llm', 'tts')
workflow.add_edge('tts', END)

memory = SqliteSaver.from_conn_string(':memory:')
app = workflow.compile(checkpointer=memory)

implementation.title: Real-time Audio Processing Loop
implementation.description: Main event loop handling WebSocket audio streams with sub-second latency
implementation.code:
import asyncio
import time

async def voice_agent_loop(app, websocket):
    config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "voice_session_1"}}
    start_time = time.time()
    
    async for message in websocket:
        if message.type == 'audio':
            # Latency budget check
            if time.time() - start_time > 0.5:
                await websocket.send('TIMEOUT')
                continue
            
            state = {'audio_chunk': message.data, 'cycle_count': 0, 'conversation_history': []}
            result = await app.ainvoke(state, config)
            
            # Stream TTS response
            await websocket.send(result['audio_output'])
            start_time = time.time()

implementation.title: Parallel Sentiment Analysis Branch
implementation.description: Concurrent safety analysis that doesn't block main response path
implementation.code:
async def safety_node(state: VoiceState) -> VoiceState:
    # Parallel sentiment check
    sentiment = 'safe' if 'harm' not in state['transcript'].lower() else 'unsafe'
    state['sentiment_score'] = 0.9 if sentiment == 'safe' else 0.1
    return state

# Add parallel branch
workflow.add_node('safety', safety_node)
workflow.add_edge('stt', 'safety')
workflow.add_edge('safety', 'llm')  # Parallel execution

# Conditional escalation
async def should_escalate(state: VoiceState) -> str:
    return 'human' if state.get('sentiment_score', 1.0) < 0.3 else 'llm'

implementation.title: Interrupt Handling and State Recovery
implementation.description: Graceful handling of user barge-in during TTS playback
implementation.code:
async def interrupt_handler(state: VoiceState, interrupt_audio: str) -> VoiceState:
    state['audio_chunk'] = interrupt_audio  # Replace current audio
    state['interrupted'] = True
    return state

# Interrupt edge
workflow.add_conditional_edges(
    'tts',
    should_interrupt,
    {'interrupt': 'stt', 'continue': END}
)

def should_interrupt(state: VoiceState) -> str:
    return 'interrupt' if state.get('pending_interrupt') else 'continue'

vexIntegration.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Catch cycle limits, latency violations, and safety issues at graph boundaries
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify, Guardrail

voice_guards = [
    Guardrail('cycle_limit', max_cycles=20),
    Guardrail('latency_budget', max_duration_ms=500),
    Guardrail('safety_filter', categories=['hate', 'violence']),
    Guardrail('coherence_check', min_similarity=0.7)
]

async def verified_voice_loop(app, websocket):
    config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "session_1"}}
    
    async for message in websocket:
        state = {'audio_chunk': message.data}
        
        result = await verify(
            app.ainvoke,
            state,
            config,
            guards=voice_guards,
            on_fail='retry'
        )
        
        await websocket.send(result['audio_output'])

vexIntegration.title: Vex Node Within Graph for Continuous Monitoring
vexIntegration.description: Inline verification at every state transition prevents error propagation
vexIntegration.code:
async def vex_verify_node(state: VoiceState) -> VoiceState:
    from vex import verify_state
    
    verified_state = await verify_state(
        state,
        guards=[
            Guardrail('state_schema', expected_keys=['transcript', 'llm_response']),
            Guardrail('content_safety', fields=['llm_response']),
            Guardrail('cycle_limit', current=state['cycle_count'], max=15)
        ]
    )
    return verified_state

# Insert after every major node
workflow.add_node('vex_verify', vex_verify_node)
workflow.add_edge('stt', 'vex_verify')
workflow.add_edge('llm', 'vex_verify')
workflow.add_edge('vex_verify', 'tts')

app = workflow.compile(checkpointer=memory)

vexIntegration.title: Latency + Safety Composite Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Combined verification catching both performance and content violations
vexIntegration.code:
async def realtime_voice_handler(app, audio_stream):
    from vex import CompositeGuardrail
    
    realtime_guard = CompositeGuardrail(
        'voice_agent',
        rules={
            'latency': lambda ctx: ctx.duration < 500,
            'safety': lambda ctx: all(score > 0.8 for score in ctx.safety_scores),
            'coherence': lambda ctx: ctx.conversation_similarity > 0.7,
            'no_loops': lambda ctx: ctx.cycles < 25
        },
        failure_policy='block_and_log'
    )
    
    start = time.time()
    result = await app.ainvoke(audio_stream)
    
    await realtime_guard.verify({
        'result': result,
        'duration': time.time() - start,
        'cycles': result['cycle_count'],
        'safety_scores': analyze_safety(result['llm_response'])
    })
    
    return result

cta.heading: Secure Your LangGraph Voice Agents
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your voice graphs to eliminate latency violations and safety failures.
